title: Pointers - a variable that holds an address
author: Prodipta Acharjee
concept: A pointer holds the memory address of another variable. & takes an address, * follows it to the value.
explanation: Every variable lives somewhere in memory. & gives you that location; a pointer is a variable built to hold one. Once a pointer p points at x, *p means 'the value at that address' - reading it gets x, writing it changes x itself. That's the power and the danger of C: you're working with the memory directly.

exercises.question: What does this print?
hints: p points at x, so *p is another name for x.

exercises.question: Which operator gives you the address of a variable?
hints: * follows a pointer; this one takes the address.

exercises.question:
To read the value a pointer points at, you put which symbol before it?

_p
hints: The same symbol used to declare the pointer.
